content: Market capitalization equals share price multiplied by outstanding shares. Large-cap typically refers to companies valued above $10 billion, mid-cap $2-10 billion, small-cap under $2 billion (thresholds vary). Size categories correlate with risk-return profiles: large-caps tend more stable with lower volatility, small-caps offer higher growth potential but greater risk. Many indices and funds categorize holdings by market cap for diversification and risk management.
